column1: # Wikidata WikiProject Heads of state and government -- indicator catalog
# This file is the single source of truth for which Wikidata "head of
# state" / "head of government" offices the Stage 2 adapter queries.
# Adding/removing an office here is the only change needed to widen or
# narrow the ingestion.
# Source: Wikidata SPARQL endpoint at https://query.wikidata.org/sparql
# License: CC0 1.0 (Public Domain Dedication). Cite per
#   docs/source-attributions.md.
# Important design notes:
# - These rows are NOT rating indicators. They are leader-identity
#   reference observations for downstream Stage 4 (leader resolver).
#   The rating_category column is therefore the pseudo-category
#   "leader_identity" (not one of the 8 scoring categories from
#   requirement §4) so a Stage 5 evidence builder can recognise the
#   catalog row as a leader-reference source rather than a scoring
#   input.
# - raw_column is the Wikidata office QID: Q30461 (head of state) or
#   Q22857062 (head of government). The Stage 2 orchestrator builds
#   a SPARQL query per office QID and persists the verbatim API
#   response.
# - raw_scale is "qid_list" because each office + country can
#   produce multiple holder rows over time (different leaders) --
#   the SPARQL response is a list of (country QID
#   start date
# - normalized_value is NULL (a leader reference is a QID
#   number).
# - higher_is_better=1 by convention (a populated office is more
#   useful than an empty one) but this has no scoring impact -- it
#   is metadata only.
# - unit is "qid" -- the cell value is a Wikidata Q-identifier
#   string.
# - country_id and leader_id are NULL at Stage 2 -- Stage 3 maps the
#   country QID to our countries table (ISO3)
#   person QID to a canonical leaders row.
# SPARQL query semantics (per-office
# country_qids subset):
#   SELECT ?country ?countryLabel ?person ?personLabel
#          ?office ?officeLabel ?start ?end ?statement WHERE {
#     VALUES ?office { wd:Q30461 wd:Q22857062 }
#     VALUES ?country { wd:Q30 wd:Q96 ... }
#     { ?person wdt:P39 wd:Q30461 . }
#     UNION
#     { ?person wdt:P39 wd:Q22857062 . }
#     ?person wdt:P27 ?country .
#     ?person p:P39 ?statement .
#     ?statement ps:P39 ?office .
#     ?statement pq:P580 ?start .
#     OPTIONAL { ?statement pq:P582 ?end }
#     SERVICE wikibase:label { bd:serviceParam wikibase:language "en" }
# The country-of-citizenship (wdt:P27) on the person is more
# reliably populated than the equivalent qualifier on the P39
# statement
column2: so the query links via the person item.

column1: # When extending this file:
#   1. Verify the office QID exists in Wikidata (e.g. via the entity
#      page). Q30461 = head of state; Q22857062 = head of
#      government; Q45371 = head of state government (where
#      distinct); additional leadership QIDs can be added.
#   2. Pick a variable_name of the form
#      wikidata_<office_slug>_held that does not collide with
#      existing entries.
#   3. Set unit="qid"
#   4. Update the SPARQL query VALUES list in the orchestrator's
#      ``build_head_of_state_government_query()`` if you want the
#      new office fetched automatically; otherwise the orchestrator's
#      office loop will pick it up from the catalog.
variable_name
wikidata_head_of_state_held
column8: Wikidata "head of state" office (Q30461) - position held (P39) by a person

column1: wikidata_head_of_government_held
column8: Wikidata "head of government" office (Q22857062) - position held (P39) by a person
